tip: Use @path/to/import syntax in CLAUDE.md to import additional files. Imported files are expanded and loaded into context at launch alongside the CLAUDE.md that references them.
Create multiple .md files in .claude/rules/ — each can have a paths: YAML frontmatter to scope rules to specific file patterns. Rules without a paths field are loaded unconditionally.

Auto memory is per-project. Each project gets its own memory directory at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/, derived from the git repository so all worktrees within the same repo share one directory.
Add .mcp.json to your project root to configure project-scoped MCP servers. This file is designed to be checked into version control, ensuring all team members have access to the same MCP tools.
Use the /memory command to list all loaded CLAUDE.md and rules files, toggle auto memory on or off, and open the auto memory folder. To make Claude remember something, just ask it in conversation.

permissions.deny rules apply to Claude's built-in file tools (Read, Edit), not to Bash subprocesses. A deny rule blocks the Read tool but does not prevent cat .env in Bash. For OS-level enforcement, enable the sandbox.
CLAUDE.md files in subdirectories are loaded on demand when Claude reads files in those directories, rather than being loaded at launch.
Use .claude/settings.local.json for personal preferences and experimentation. Claude Code will configure git to ignore this file when it is created.
Press Esc twice to open the rewind menu, which shows each prompt from your session. You can restore code, conversation, or both to any previous point, or summarize from a selected message forward.
Use /btw to ask a quick question without adding to the conversation history. It runs independently even while Claude is processing, and answers only from existing context (no tool access).
Add "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/claude-code-settings.json" to settings.json to enable autocomplete and inline validation in VS Code, Cursor, or any editor that supports JSON schema.
Use --bare with claude -p to skip auto-discovery of hooks, skills, plugins, MCP servers, auto memory, and CLAUDE.md. Useful for CI and scripts where you need the same result on every machine.
Codex uses config.toml (not YAML or JSON) for configuration. Set model, approval policy, and sandbox settings there.
At each directory level, Codex checks AGENTS.override.md before AGENTS.md and loads only the first match — use it for personal overrides that replace the team file at that level.
Codex walks from the project root (detected via .git) to your current directory, loading one instruction file per level (AGENTS.override.md, AGENTS.md, or a configured fallback).
Set project_doc_fallback_filenames in config.toml to make Codex read custom instruction filenames when AGENTS.md is missing.

Codex uses .gitignore to filter the @ file picker, but gitignored files can still be read by the agent via tools and shell commands. A .codexignore feature has been requested but has no official timeline.
Define named profiles in config.toml under [profiles.<name>] and switch with codex -p fast or codex -p careful — each can override model, sandbox mode, and approval policy.
Override any config key at runtime with -c key=value using dotted paths (e.g., -c model=o3 or -c shell_environment_policy.inherit=all) — no file edits needed.

Codex can strip env vars matching KEY, SECRET, or TOKEN from agent-spawned commands, but this is disabled by default. Enable it with ignore_default_excludes = false under [shell_environment_policy] in config.toml.
Gemini CLI scans subdirectories for context files, limited to 200 directories by default. Configure this with the context.discoveryMaxDirs setting in settings.json.
Increase context.discoveryMaxDirs in settings.json if Gemini isn't finding your subdirectory GEMINI.md files in large projects.
The context.fileName setting in settings.json lets you change the default context file name — it accepts a single string or an array of filenames (e.g., ["AGENTS.md", "CONTEXT.md", "GEMINI.md"]).
Use .geminiignore in your project root (gitignore-style patterns) to exclude files from tools that support this feature, such as the @ file-sharing command.
Gemini CLI loads .env files from the current directory upward to the project root, or ~/.env as a fallback. Variables in .gemini/.env are never excluded by the default env-var exclusion filters.
Use the /memory command in Gemini CLI to dynamically add or refresh persistent context within a session.
Create .toml files in .gemini/commands/ for custom slash commands. Use !{git diff --staged} for shell injection and @{docs/guide.md} for file injection in prompts.

Set context.memoryBoundaryMarkers in settings.json to control where upward GEMINI.md discovery stops. The default is [".git"]; add markers like "package.json" to prevent traversal above sub-project roots in monorepos.
GEMINI.md supports modular imports via @./path/to/file.md syntax — with circular-import detection and up to 5 levels of nesting.
.cursor/rules/ files (.md or .mdc) support frontmatter with globs: and alwaysApply: fields to control how rules are applied.

Rules in .cursor/rules/ have four modes: Always Apply (every session), Apply Intelligently (agent decides based on description), Apply to Specific Files (glob match), or Apply Manually (@-mentioned in chat).
.cursorignore blocks files from semantic search, Tab, Agent, Inline Edit, and @-mention references — but not from terminal commands or MCP server tools.
Use .cursorindexingignore to exclude files from codebase searches while still keeping them accessible to AI features.
Cursor reads AGENTS.md as a simple alternative to .cursor/rules/, with support for nested AGENTS.md files in subdirectories.
Configure MCP servers per-project in .cursor/mcp.json or globally in ~/.cursor/mcp.json.
Press Cmd+Right (Mac) or Ctrl+Right to accept a Tab suggestion word-by-word. Separately, after accepting any Tab suggestion, press Tab again to jump to the predicted next edit location.
Cursor Tab predicts cross-file edits when changes in one file need updates in another. A portal window appears at the bottom of the editor when a cross-file jump is available.
Use @Docs > Add new doc in Cursor Chat to index external documentation for reference. Manage indexed docs under Cursor Settings > Features > Docs.
Antigravity stores its data under ~/.gemini/antigravity/ — including conversations/, brain/, global_workflows/, and mcp_config.json.
When both GEMINI.md and AGENTS.md exist in a directory, Antigravity gives GEMINI.md higher precedence.
Enable 'Load nested AGENTS.md files' in Antigravity Settings to pick up instruction files in subdirectories.
Use .agent/rules/ in your project for organizing multiple workspace rule files that Antigravity loads automatically.
Antigravity supports .agent/workflows/ for step-by-step automation recipes that you can trigger on demand with / as you interact with the agent.
Add // turbo above a workflow step to auto-execute that command without approval prompts, or // turbo-all anywhere in the file to skip prompts for all steps.
Set the Terminal Command Auto Execution policy to 'Request Review' in Antigravity Settings for a positive security model where only explicitly allow-listed commands can run without approval.
Create .github/instructions/*.instructions.md files with applyTo frontmatter for path-specific Copilot instructions.
.github/prompts/*.prompt.md files are reusable prompt templates you can reference in Copilot Chat via / followed by the prompt name.
Copilot content exclusion is managed through GitHub organization/repository admin settings, not a local ignore file.
GitHub Copilot CLI stores MCP server config in ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json. Configure it directly or use the /mcp add command.
Copilot's coding agent reads AGENTS.md files, alongside .github/copilot-instructions.md, CLAUDE.md, and GEMINI.md.
Add copilot-setup-steps.yml to .github/workflows/ to preinstall tools and dependencies in the coding agent's environment before it starts working.
Use #read/terminalLastCommand in Copilot Chat to attach the last terminal command and its output as context — no copy-pasting needed.
Use github.copilot.chat.commitMessageGeneration.instructions in VS Code settings to enforce Conventional Commits or any format for the sparkle-button commit messages.
AGENTS.md is supported by Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Copilot, and other coding agents as a shared instruction file format.

Codex concatenates instruction files from the project root down to your working directory. Files closer to your current directory override earlier guidance because they appear later in the combined prompt.
Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md, not AGENTS.md. If your repo uses AGENTS.md for other agents, create a CLAUDE.md that imports it with @AGENTS.md so both tools read the same instructions.
